summary: Improved address entry in the passenger order form per the user's request to add conveniences and use Yandex Maps capabilities. taxi/templates/client/home.html: replaced the static from/to address <span> chips (which only updated via tap-on-map reverse geocode) with real editable <input> fields wired to ymaps.SuggestView — typing now shows live Yandex address autocomplete (biased to the Tashkent bounding box), selecting a suggestion forward-geocodes and drops the pin automatically, so users no longer have to precisely tap a small map. Also added a swap (⬆️⬇️) button to flip from/to in one tap. Found and fixed a layout bug while implementing this: the address rows were originally nested inside the same ios-card as the map, which has overflow-hidden for rounded corners — that would have silently clipped the Yandex suggestion dropdown. Split it into two separate cards (map card stays overflow-hidden; a new address-input card below it does not) so the suggestion list can render fully. Verified the template still renders correctly (200, contains the new from-input/SuggestView/swapAddresses elements) via a Django test-client smoke test against sqlite.
current_status: Passenger MVP now has Yandex-backed address autocomplete plus map-tap and current-location entry, all three ways converging on the same _fromLat/_fromLng/_toLat/_toLng state used by order submission. No backend changes were needed for this round (client_views.py/client_urls.py untouched). Same pre-launch checklist as before: apply migration 0043_client_user on production Postgres, build/sign a release APK, on-device testing, and the deferred phase (chat, SOS, push, passenger-rates-driver).
